settings.title: Excess Oil Production
settings.text: Sebum build-up creates an environment prone to blockages.

settings.title: Cellular Congestion
settings.text: Dead skin cells trap oil within the pore lining.

settings.title: Bacterial Imbalance
settings.text: Trapped debris leads to visible inflammation and breakouts.

settings.title: Acne Control Routine
settings.description:
A unified system engineered to restore clarity to acne-prone skin.

How and when to apply this routine:

  • AM: Cleanse to remove overnight oil. Apply sunscreen to protect against UV inflammation.
  • PM: Cleanse to remove daily buildup. Apply moisturiser to repair the skin barrier without clogging pores.

settings.text: Baseline purification. Excess surface oil is reduced.
Sebum regulation. Pores appear less congested.
Sustained clarity. Skin barrier function is optimized.
Cleanse post-workout to prevent sweat buildup.
Apply SPF daily to prevent hyperpigmentation.

settings.question: What is the best simple skincare routine for acne-prone skin?
settings.answer: The most effective acne routine is one you can maintain consistently. Our Acne Control Routine provides the three non-negotiable steps: a clarifying cleanser to remove congestion, a non-comedogenic moisturiser to repair the barrier, and a daily sunscreen to prevent post-acne dark spots.

settings.question: How and when should I apply an acne routine?
settings.answer: Consistency is key. In the morning, cleanse your face and follow immediately with sunscreen to protect against environmental triggers. In the evening, cleanse again to remove daily buildup, then apply moisturiser to hydrate and repair your skin overnight.

settings.question: How long does it take for an acne routine to work?
settings.answer: While surface oil reduction can be felt within the first week, clinical cellular turnover takes approximately 28 days. You should expect to see a visible reduction in congestion between weeks 4 and 6, with sustained clarity developing by week 8.

settings.question: Do I need to buy individual ingredients or a complete routine?
settings.answer: You do not need to become a skincare chemist. Mixing individual active ingredients often leads to barrier damage and worsened breakouts. We engineered the Acne Control Routine as a complete, synchronized system so the formulation chemistry is already solved for you.

settings.hero_body: You don't need a degree in skincare. You need a system that works. We eliminated the guesswork to deliver a synchronized, non-comedogenic routine designed specifically for acne-prone skin.
